ItacWorkItem.ShortTitle: Il Diamerone di M. Valerio Marcellino
ItacWorkItem.LongTitle: Ove con vive ragioni si mostra, la morte non esser quel male, che`l senso si persuade. Con una dotta, e giudiciosa lettera, over discorso intorno alla lingua volgare.
ItacWorkItem.Notes: Diamerone is a philosophical treatise, which includes a letter on the subject of vernacular Italian. The editor`s letter of dedication suggests that the text was written about 4 years before. In this work, the author claims to be reporting the converstations held in the house of Domenico Venier. The dialogue includes one monsignor Giuliano, Girolamo Molino, Giorgio Gradenigo, Bernardo Tasso, Dionigi Atanagi, Sperone Speroni, and the young men Luigi Belegno, Sebastiano Magno, monsignor Girolamo Fenarolo, Antonio Diedo, Pietro Badoaro, Celio Magno and others. See the summary by Lorenzo Carpane`, `Marcellino, Valerio`, in DBI.

Essay.EssayTitle: Lettera over discorso di M. Valerio Marcellino intorno alla lingua volgare

ItacWorkItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
